articles.title: Week 9: Writing for an Audience That Cannot See
articles.excerpt: The AI agent reading your article is not reading it. It is parsing it. A practical Founder Friday on what changes editorially when half your audience cannot see headlines, layout, or images. Structure, voice, and the unexpected convergence: writing well for agents is just writing well.
articles.author: Ripper

articles.title: Week 8: We Stopped Waiting for AdSense. Here's What That Bought Us.
articles.excerpt: Four weeks ago I was still waiting on Google. Then I stopped. Stopping was the most important monetization decision this project has made. A Founder Friday on why building dependence on a permission-granting gatekeeper is the wrong shape for an AI-era publisher.
articles.author: Ripper

articles.title: Week 7: The Cost of Saying Yes
articles.excerpt: Every adjacent vertical that pattern-matches to existing infrastructure looks free and almost never is. The hardest founder skill is not knowing what to build. It is knowing what to refuse. A Founder Friday on scope discipline as the load-bearing decision of running a federation.
articles.author: Ripper

articles.title: Week 6: Notes from the Road, and Why a Federation is Easier Than One Big Site
articles.excerpt: Founder Friday from the road. A counterintuitive argument: running four specialized sites is lower cognitive load than running one sprawling one. Why the federation model is underrated for small teams, and how the shared infrastructure pays back the structural cost.
articles.author: Ripper

articles.title: Week 5: Going Quiet to Ship a Network
articles.excerpt: Six weeks dark on the blog while TerminalFeed quietly turned into a federation. VR.org, HangryHQ, and TensorFeed now share data, payments, and security infra. Here is what shipped, and why the network matters more than any one site.
articles.author: Ripper

articles.title: bitcointicker.co Alternatives: The Real-Time Crypto Dashboard Landscape in 2026
articles.excerpt: bitcointicker.co is the OG minimalist BTC ticker. We mapped its modern alternatives, from pure price pages to terminal-style multi-feed dashboards, and where each one fits.
articles.author: Pulse
